description: This figure compares experimental and simulated dynamic recrystallization (DRX) microstructure evolution of low-alloy steel under constant and transiently increasing strain rates at 950℃. Subplot (a) shows EBSD-reconstructed grain orientation maps and corresponding grain orientation spread (GOS) maps at 0.9 strain for constant 0.1 s⁻¹ strain rate and transient strain rate increase at εI=0.2/0.4, with DRX fractions of 95.0%, 94.4%, 96.3%, and average grain sizes of 28.89±3.09μm, 31.93±2.03μm, 37.16±2.26μm respectively. Subplot (b) compares reconstructed microstructures at 0.4/0.5 strain for transient strain rate increase from 0.01 s⁻¹ to 0.1 s⁻¹ and constant 0.1 s⁻¹, with average grain sizes ranging from 50.53±5.62μm to 65.33±5.30μm. Subplots (c-e) present 3D cellular automaton simulated microstructure evolution under different strain rate paths, showing consistent DRX fraction increase and grain size refinement trend with rising strain, matching experimental observations well to validate the 3D CA model accuracy for DRX prediction under varying deformation conditions.
